Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for underlying causes.
